item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
The AW signature picture was licensed as NC by Aelanna, which is not compliant with CC BY-SA 3.0. As such, it has since been removed and replaced with text.

Edit: new image made by Laneous, licensed under BY-SA 3.0.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Turn back the clock you say? Would a time-travelling boat work? Also, this is quite interesting and I hope I can see more of this Professor AW

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's nothing to the SCP beyond a transparent attempt at building intrigue for a pet GoI. This being acknowledged in the authorpost does not make the article itself any more interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Breach Incident 1851-Delta-Four-Alpha

Shouldn't it be "1408-Delta-Four-Alpha"? Since you, well, changed the number?

E: Page King yay.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This does seem like it has an interesting story to tell, but it seemed a little chaotic. I like the idea behind this, though. It just seems a bit lackluster to me. The heart of the SCP is not the item itself, but the story here. For these reasons, I have a neutral vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This would be interesting if we could actually see the effects in motion, but this doesn't do that. It's all buildup with no payoff. The Professor AW note at the end feels like a cop-out. Also, like Lumancer said, this is totally neutralized, but instead of changing the object class it'd be better if you changed it to be working, and added something that was actually interesting to it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is an exceptionally small nit, but isn't this technically neutralized?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the larger concept and support your attempt to add to canon. I picture AW as a sort of Moriarty for Blackwood, not many direct confrontations, but a lot of crossed paths wre Blackwood inadvertently foils plots or has to clean up aftermath.

But the item in and of itself is just sort of weak. Partly because the note is right, this should be archived because it no longer does anything anomalous. Partly because Spider Robinson did it better, and the steampunk spin adds nothing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually SCP-276 could " turn back the clock" unless this article existed before SCP 276 was discovered and /or you are using FAQ Question 12 Paragraph 2.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this a lot, especially the execution. The wax cylinders remind me of Edison's recording devices.

+1

P.S. I wouldn't mind attributing 1875 to Professor AW…if you think it fits with the character you're developing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am counting down the hours until Lord Blackwood meets Professor AW.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1408 is a movie based on a short story by Stephen King.

Downvote.

(that's sarcasm)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Due to popular demand, I have moved this from the 1851 slot to 1408. :I

HOPE YOU'RE ALL HAPPY NOW.

It IS, however, still suppose to be AA #1851-D-4, so don't complain about that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This article does something I dislike, meaningful SCP numbers. No upvote.

Different number now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I assumed the reason it was named like that was because the RSSCPAA catalogued their objects according to year discovered, and any subsequently discovered artifacts would be named SCP-1851-a, -1851-b and so on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a 150 year old helmet that could fuck with someone's mind if repaired, and REALLY fuck with someone's mind if completed. Personally, I like that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
AWwwwww
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not a bad story overall.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I, for one, am amused and tickled that an organization I made up for a Lord Blackwood story is wiggling its way into popular headcanon, but it really should be "Royal Foundation for the Security, Containment, and Protection of Anomalous Items and Phantasmagoria". :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know what is better than a canon-wielding, new character establishing, real history referencing clever article? A canon-wielding, new character establishing, real history referencing clever article that is based around an object that is actually interesting on itself.

No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I agree, can the date be changed?

Regardless, up-voted as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I definitely want to see where you go with this IOI. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't think I like the inclusion of the the year 1851 in the actual article text; it feels too much like leaning on the fourth wall. Nevertheless, upvoted, and I endorse this new IOI.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The incident occurred before the invention of video or audio surveillance, and all we have is a hundred-year-old handwritten note supposedly written by someone who should have been dead of old age.

That one segment of text feels a little 'hand-wavey' to me, otherwise I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Royal Society for the Security, Containment, and Protection of Anomalous Artifacts

I hurt my fingers rushing to the upvote button when I read that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it in sandbox, and I like it now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Alright, time to fess up.

This is the first in a series of attempts to establish a new Individual of Interest, Professor AW. If this works out, then I intend to edit a few of my previous SCPs to be attributed to this new canon.
